bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting my maximum-utility bid. The opponent opened with all valueC, which is the lowest valid value for me on every issue, so I do not yet see evidence of concession or a reason to move away from my strongest preferences.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid while making only a small concession to avoid simply repeating my prior all-valueA offer. The opponent has moved only slightly from all valueC to valueB on issueD, raising its utility to me only marginally, so I should not concede on my high-weight issues yet.

I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.92 because it is still very early at t=0.04 and the opponent has only made minimal movement, improving my utility from 0.20 to about 0.241 by conceding only issueD to valueB. Their repeated offers remain very unfavorable to me, so I will keep my high-weight issues A, B, and C at valueA while making small concessions on lower-weight issues to avoid repeating my last bid.

At t=0.06 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.88 while making limited concessions only on lower-weight issues. The opponent’s offers remain very low for me and mostly fixed at valueC, with only small movement on issueD/issueF, so I will hold firm on the high-importance issues A, B, and C while conceding moderately on D/E/F.

At t=0.08 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making only a limited concession on a low-weight issue. The opponent’s offers remain very low for me and mostly hold firm on valueC, with only small movement on issueD/F, so I will keep my high-weight issues A/B/C at valueA and concede issueE to valueC where they have consistently signaled interest.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.80 because it is early in the negotiation (t=0.10), so there is no need to concede on my highest-weight issues yet. The opponent has stayed firm on valueC for issueA/issueB/issueC and mostly issueE, while only varying low-weight issueD/issueF, so I am matching their latest low-importance signals on D/E/F while holding A/B/C at my preferred valueA.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.84 by holding firm on the most important issues A, B, and C while conceding on lower-weight issues D, E, and F. Since it is still early at t=0.12, there is no need for major concessions, but the opponent has repeatedly offered valueB on issueD and most recently valueB on issueE, so I am incorporating those signals while avoiding a repeat of my last bid.

I am targeting a still-high utility around 0.80 by holding firm on the high-weight issues A, B, and C while conceding on lower-weight issues. At t=0.14 it is still early, and the opponent has not conceded on my important issues, but their bids suggest they may care about getting valueC on several issues, so I am offering concessions mainly on D and F while varying from my previous bid.

I am targeting about 0.80 utility by holding firm on the high-weight issues A, B, and C while varying concessions on the lower-weight issues. At t=0.16 it is still early, and the opponent’s valid offers remain very low for me and mostly fixed on valueC for the important issues, so I should not make major concessions yet. This bid avoids repeating my immediately previous offer while giving valueC on some low-weight issues the opponent has repeatedly proposed.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.82 because it is still early at t=0.18, so there is no need to concede on my most important issues A, B, and C. The opponent has held firm on valueC for A/B/C and mostly valueC on E/F, with some flexibility around D, so I concede mainly on lower-weight issues while keeping my core valueA positions.

At t=0.20 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid for me while conceding only on the lowest-weight issues. The opponent has held firm on valueC for issueA, issueB, and issueC, with only small movement on issueD/E/F, so I will hold my high-importance issues at valueA and offer valueC on the low-importance issues to explore a tradeoff.

I am targeting a utility around 0.80 by holding firm on the high-weight issues A, B, and C while conceding on the lower-weight E and F. At t=0.22 there is still time, and the opponent has not meaningfully conceded on my important issues, but they repeatedly offer issueD:valueB, so I can use that as a modest compromise without dropping too far.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.84 because time is still early at t=0.24, so I should hold firm on the most important issues A, B, and C. The opponent has consistently demanded valueC on those high-weight issues, but has shown some movement on lower-weight issues D and E, including their latest offer with issueD:valueB and issueE:valueB, so I mirror those concessions while keeping issueF at valueC to improve acceptability.

At t=0.26 it is still relatively early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making concessions only on lower-weight issues. The opponent has consistently demanded valueC on issueA/B/C, which are my most important issues, so I will hold those at valueA and concede on issueD/E/F where the utility loss is smaller and where they have shown some movement to valueB on D and E.

I am still targeting a high-utility bid around 0.80 because we are only at t=0.28, so I should not concede on the high-weight issues A, B, and C yet. The opponent has remained firm on valueC for issueA/B/C and has often proposed valueC on issueE/F, so I am conceding on low-weight issueE and issueF while holding my main priorities.

At t=0.30 I am still targeting a high-utility bid around 0.80, since there is time left and the opponent has not meaningfully conceded on the major issues A, B, and C. Their latest offer keeps A/B/C at valueC but shows willingness around issueE=valueB and issueD=valueC, so I concede mainly on lower-weight issues while holding my most important issues at valueA.

At t=0.32 I am still targeting a high-utility bid, keeping my most important issues A, B, and C at valueA while conceding on lower-weight issues. The opponent has consistently held issueA/B/C at valueC but has signaled flexibility or preference around issueD=valueB, issueE=valueB, and issueF=valueC, so I match those lower-weight positions to improve acceptability without giving up my core value.

I am targeting about 0.80 utility by holding firm on the high-weight issues A, B, and C while conceding on lower-weight issues. At t=0.34 there is some time pressure to show movement, but the opponent’s offers still give me very low utility and they have not conceded on A/B/C, so I should not give up my main value yet.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.76 while recognizing the opponent has just made a meaningful concession by moving issueA and issueB from valueC to valueB. Since time is only at 0.36, I will still hold firm on my two most important issues, but I will concede on issueC to valueB and keep flexibility on lower-weight issues to test whether this tradeoff is acceptable.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.72 by holding firm on high-weight issueA and issueB while keeping issueC at valueB and conceding lower-weight issues E and F to valueC. At t=0.38 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept the opponent’s low-utility pattern; their recent offers show modest concession on issueA and issueB to valueB, but they remain firm on issueC/valueC and low issues mostly valueC. With no messages, I rely only on the offer trend and make a reciprocal but limited concession from my prior bid.

I am targeting a moderate mid-game utility around 0.68, conceding on lower-priority and opponent-signaled issues while holding firm on my top priorities issueA and issueB. The opponent has recently improved from all valueC to valueB on issueA and issueB, showing some concession, but they still strongly favor valueC on issueC/F and valueB or valueC on D/E, so this bid tests a compromise without dropping too far at t=0.40.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.64, preserving my highest-weight issues A and B at valueA while conceding on issueC and lower-weight issues to move closer to the opponent. The opponent has recently improved from all valueC to valueB on issueA and issueB, but they still hold issueC at valueC and mostly prefer valueC/valueB on low-weight issues, so this bid tests whether keeping A/B is acceptable while making meaningful concessions elsewhere.

I am targeting a utility around 0.56: still well above reservation, but with a meaningful mid-game concession. The opponent has recently improved from all valueC to valueB on issueA and issueB, while staying firm on issueC/valueC, so I am holding my most important issueA at valueA but conceding issueB and the lower-weight issues toward their pattern.

I am targeting a mid-game utility a little above 0.5, conceding mainly on lower-weight issues while still holding issueA at my top value because it is my most important issue. The opponent has improved from all valueC to valueB on issueA/issueB and often issueD, but their latest offer is still only about 0.446 utility to me, so at t=0.46 I should not drop all the way to it. To avoid repeating my last bid, I concede issueD further while keeping issueB at the compromise valueB and issueC/issueE/issueF at valueC.
